```json
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Datum scheme misunderstood: part being checked off wrong surface (DOC - datum of convenience or incorrect datum feature)",
    "Inspection/gauging setup does not match the engineering datum reference frame defined on the drawing",
    "All measurements taken from this setup are invalid and do not verify part conformance",
    "Risk of accepting out-of-tolerance parts or rejecting good parts due to incorrect datum alignment"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop current inspection/gauging activity immediately",
    "Review drawing GD&T and datum feature symbols (A, B, C) per ASME Y14.5",
    "Re-establish datum reference frame on correct surfaces/features",
    "Re-train operator on datum scheme interpretation and gauge setup",
    "Re-inspect all parts previously checked using the wrong datum setup",
    "Update work instructions / gauge instructions to clarify datum setup"
  ],
  "rationale": "The observed condition is a datum scheme misunderstanding where the part is being checked from the wrong surface. In GD&T, datums establish the theoretical reference frame from which all other features are measured. Checking from the wrong datum invalidates the measurement and means the part condition is not verified against the drawing. Because the work is rough-complete and the defect affects quality verification, this is a fail condition with high risk of non-conforming product escaping or good parts being scrapped.",
  "references": [
    "ASME Y14.5 (basic) - datum reference frame and datum feature symbols",
    "Gauge instructions - proper setup and datum alignment procedures"
  ]
}
```
